{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Austrian Attack:Dragon| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd| | |rd|= |pd|pd| | |pd|pd|bd|pd|= | | | |pd| |nd|pd| |= | | |pd| | | | | |= | | | |pl|pl|pl| | |= | | |nl| | |nl| | |= |pl|pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl| |rl|= || }} =Austrian Attack: Dragon= ===5...c5=== Black strikes at the center before White can make a move with it. His move may seem antipositional, as after 6.dxc5 dxc5 7.Qxd8+ Kxd8 8.Be3 b6 9.0-0-0+ Bd7 gives White an excellent game, due to Black's uncastled king and poor pawn position. However, Black can get a favorable [[Wikipedia:pawn structure|pawn structure]] by using a tactical trick. The reason for the variation's nickname is for the excellent pawn structure that Black can get out of it, the [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c5/2. Nf3/2...d6/3. d4/3...cxd4/4. Nxd4/4...Nf6/5. Nc3/5...g6|Sicilian Dragon]]. ==Theory table== :1.e4 d6 2.d4 Nf6 3.Nc3 g6 4.f4 Bg7 5.Nf3 c5
